subject: math
title: Grade 3 Math — EOG Practice Test 4
instructions: Read each question carefully. Choose the best answer. For numeric entry questions, write only the number. For fill-in-the-blank equations, write the number that goes in the box.

questions.questionText: The array below shows rows of buttons. Which multiplication fact does it show?
questions.explanation: There are 4 rows with 6 dots in each row. 4 × 6 = 24.

questions.questionText: What is 7 × 8? Write only the number.
What is 54 ÷ 6?
Mr. Lopez has 32 crayons. He puts them into boxes of 8. How many boxes does he fill?
questions.explanation: 32 ÷ 8 = 4 boxes.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
Which number do these base-ten blocks show?
questions.explanation: 3 hundreds, 6 tens, and 4 ones make 300 + 60 + 4 = 364.

questions.questionText: A bar is split into 3 equal parts. What fraction names ONE part?
questions.explanation: When a whole is split into 3 equal parts, each part is 1/3 of the whole.

questions.questionText: What is 86 rounded to the nearest ten?
questions.explanation: 86 is between 80 and 90. The ones digit is 6, so round up to 90.

questions.questionText: What fraction of the circle is shaded?
questions.explanation: 3 of the 4 equal parts are shaded, so 3/4 is shaded.

questions.questionText: What time does the clock show?
questions.explanation: The hour hand is just past 4, and the minute hand points to 3, which means 15 minutes. The time is 4:15.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
How many sides does this pentagon have?
questions.explanation: A pentagon has 5 sides.

questions.questionText: What fraction of this bar is shaded?
questions.explanation: 2 of the 6 equal parts are shaded, so 2/6 is shaded.

questions.questionText: What is the area of this rectangle?
questions.explanation: Area = width × height = 5 × 4 = 20 square cm.

questions.questionText: What is the perimeter of this rectangle?
questions.explanation: Perimeter = 8 + 3 + 8 + 3 = 22 cm.

questions.questionText: A class has 8 students. 3 of them wear glasses. What fraction of the class wears glasses?
questions.explanation: 3 out of 8 students wear glasses, so 3/8 of the class wears glasses.

questions.questionText: A library had 600 books. Students borrowed 248 books. How many books are left?
There are 8 baskets. Each basket holds 6 apples. How many apples are there in all? Write only the number.
questions.explanation: 8 × 6 = 48 apples.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
Which fraction does the point show on the number line?
questions.explanation: The line from 0 to 1 is split into 4 equal parts. The point is at the 1st mark, which is 1/4.

questions.questionText: Which fraction is equivalent to 1/2?
questions.explanation: 1/2 of a bar shades the same amount as 4/8 of an equal bar. So 1/2 = 4/8.

questions.questionText: Which comparison is true?
questions.explanation: When a whole is split into fewer parts, each part is bigger. Halves are bigger than sixths, so 1/2 > 1/6.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
A rug is 7 ft wide and 6 ft long. What is its area, in square feet? Write only the number.
questions.explanation: Area = 7 × 6 = 42 square feet.

questions.questionText: The bar graph shows books read by each student. How many more books did Tara read than Sam?
questions.explanation: Tara read 18 and Sam read 10. 18 − 10 = 8 more books.

questions.questionText: Which fraction does the point show on the number line?
questions.explanation: The line from 0 to 1 is split into 3 equal parts. The point is at the 2nd mark, which is 2/3.

questions.questionText: Recess starts at 10:50 and lasts 25 minutes. What time does recess end?
questions.explanation: From 10:50 add 10 minutes to reach 11:00, then 15 more minutes makes 11:15. So 10:50 + 25 minutes = 11:15.

questions.questionText: Select all fractions that equal a whole number (1).
questions.explanation: 4/4, 3/3, and 6/6 each shade the whole, so each equals 1. But 2/4 only shades half, so it is not 1.

questions.questionText: A teacher made 6 packs of pencils. Each pack had 9 pencils. She handed out 30 pencils. How many pencils are left?
questions.explanation: First find the total: 6 × 9 = 54 pencils. Then subtract: 54 − 30 = 24 pencils left.

questions.questionText: Each comic book costs $7. Maya buys 4 comic books and pays with a $40 bill. How many dollars of change does she get back? Write only the number.
A rectangle has a length of 9 cm and a width of 4 cm. Which statement is true about this rectangle?
options: Its area is 36 square cm and its perimeter is 26 cm
Its area is 13 square cm and its perimeter is 26 cm
Its area is 36 square cm and its perimeter is 36 cm
Its area is 26 square cm and its perimeter is 36 cm
questions.explanation: Area = 9 × 4 = 36 square cm. Perimeter = 9 + 4 + 9 + 4 = 26 cm.

questions.questionText: Ben ate 3/6 of a sandwich and Lily ate 1/2 of the same size sandwich. Who ate more?
questions.explanation: 3/6 and 1/2 cover the same amount because 3/6 = 1/2. They ate the same amount.

questions.questionText: In the picture graph, each star stands for 4 tickets sold. How many tickets did Grade 3 and Grade 4 sell together? Write only the number.
questions.explanation: Grade 3: 5 × 4 = 20 tickets. Grade 4: 3 × 4 = 12 tickets. Together: 20 + 12 = 32 tickets.

questions.questionText: A garden has 8 rows with 5 plants in each row. Then 4 plants die. Which expression shows the number of plants left?
questions.explanation: Multiply rows by plants per row, then subtract the dead plants: 8 × 5 − 4 = 40 − 4 = 36.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
Fill in the blank to make the equation true.
questions.explanation: 7 × 6 = 42. Then 42 − 12 = 30, so the box is 30 because 30 + 12 = 42.

questions.questionText: Select all expressions that equal 24.
Select all fractions that are equal to 1/4.
questions.explanation: 2/8 = 1/4 and 3/12 = 1/4. But 3/4 and 1/8 are not equal to 1/4.

questions.questionText: Select all numbers that are even.
questions.explanation: A number is even if its ones digit is 0, 2, 4, 6, or 8. 38 and 56 are even. 71 and 95 are odd.

questions.questionText: Select all fractions that are greater than 1/2.
questions.explanation: 3/4, 2/3, and 5/8 are each more than one half. But 1/4 is less than 1/2.
